cases.description: The canonical worked example from Vorne's 'Fast Guide to OEE'. An 8-hour shift (480 min) with 60 min of breaks, 47 min of down time, an ideal rate of 60 pieces/min, 19271 total pieces and 423 rejects.
cases.source: Vorne Industries, 'The Fast Guide to OEE'.

cases.description: A CNC machining shift (all times in seconds). Planned production time 26100 s (435 min), 3690 s (61.5 min) of stoppages, ideal cycle 60 s/piece, 312 pieces of which 298 are first-time-right (9 rework + 5 scrap = 14 not good). Published A 85.86%, P 83.54%, Q 95.51%, OEE 68.52%.
cases.source: TeepTrak, 'How to Calculate OEE: Formula + Worked Example'.

cases.description: Hand-derived: 480 min planned, 80 min down (run 400), ideal cycle 0.5 min/piece, 700 pieces all good. A = 400/480, P = (0.5*700)/400, Q = 1, OEE = (700*0.5)/480.
World-class benchmark factors (availability 90%, performance 95%, quality 99.9%), giving OEE just above the 85% world-class level.
cases.source: Nakajima, S. (1988), Introduction to TPM, Productivity Press (world-class benchmark: A>=90%, P>=95%, Q>=99.9%, OEE>=85%).
